stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Cajamarca is a province in northern Peru.
  • Provinces are administrative divisions responsible for coordinating local services such as public transportation.
  • Cajamarca covers an area of roughly 2,979 km².
  • Public transportation is one example of a service provinces help organize.
  • The Cajamarca Region is divided into 13 provinces.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the size of Cajamarca. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Cajamarca is one of 13 provinces that make up the Cajamarca Region of northern Peru.
Public transportation is one example of a service that provinces such as Cajamarca help coordinate.
The province of Cajamarca, in northern Peru, covers an area of roughly 2,979 km².
Cajamarca—an administrative division in northern Peru—coordinates many local services for its residents.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes the size of Cajamarca, noting that it covers an area of roughly 2,979 km².

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence locates Cajamarca within a larger region but doesn’t emphasize its size. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence gives an example of a service provinces coordinate; it doesn’t emphasize Cajamarca’s size. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence provides information about Cajamarca, it doesn’t emphasize its size.
